tools.name: gortex__get_callers
tools.description: Returns all callers of a function without reading source. Use instead of Grep when you need to know who calls a function.

exclude_tests.description: Drop callers originating in test functions (set true when you want production callers only)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

min_tier.description: Filter edges by minimum confidence tier. Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__get_class_hierarchy
tools.description: Returns the inheritance subgraph around a type, interface, or method. Walks EdgeExtends + EdgeImplements + EdgeComposes for type nodes and EdgeOverrides for method nodes — the same graph data find_implementations and find_overrides expose, but as a multi-hop tree so an agent gets the whole chain (parents → root, children → leaves) in one call. Use before refactoring an OO hierarchy or to answer 'what does this class inherit from / who subclasses it'.

depth.description: Max hops to walk in each direction (default: 5, hard cap: 64)

direction.description: 'up' (parents/interfaces this extends or implements; methods this overrides), 'down' (subtypes / implementers / overriders), or 'both' (default)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

id.description: Seed node ID — a type, interface, or method

include_methods.description: When true and the seed/visited node is a type or interface, also include its methods (via EdgeMemberOf) and walk the EdgeOverrides chain rooted at each method.

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

min_tier.description: Filter edges by minimum confidence tier. Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__get_cluster
tools.description: Returns the immediate neighbourhood around a node — all symbols it touches and that touch it. Useful for understanding a module's coupling before refactoring.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

id.description: Optional community ID (e.g. community-0). When set, returns full details of that community instead of the list.

tools.name: gortex__get_dependencies
tools.description: Returns what a symbol or file depends on — imports, calls, type references — without reading any files. Use before editing to understand incoming contracts.

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__get_diagnostics
tools.description: Returns the most recent LSP diagnostics for a file. The file is auto-opened on the server if it isn't already, and the call optionally waits for the next publishDiagnostics burst.

project.description: Filter to repositories in a specific project

repo.description: Filter to a specific repository prefix

wait.description: Block for the next publishDiagnostics (default: false)

tools.name: gortex__get_edit_plan
tools.description: Given symbols you plan to change, returns a dependency-ordered list of files and symbols to edit — definitions first, then implementations, then callers, then tests. Eliminates manual dependency reasoning. Use before any multi-file refactor.

ids.description: Comma-separated list of symbol IDs to change

tools.name: gortex__get_editing_context
tools.description: The primary tool to call before editing any file. Returns all symbols defined in the file, their signatures, direct dependencies, and immediate callers — everything needed to code without reading raw source lines.

compress_bodies.description: Also return a source_compressed view of the whole file with every function/method body replaced by a `{ /* N lines elided */ }` stub. Signatures, imports, types, and comments are preserved verbatim. Roughly 60-70% fewer tokens than raw source. Composable with format:"gcx". Default: false.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

if_none_match.description: ETag from a previous response — returns not_modified if content unchanged

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

max_tokens.description: Cap the marshaled response at approximately this many tokens (3.5 bytes/token heuristic; composable with max_bytes — tighter wins). Use when you have a token budget instead of a byte budget. Omit for no cap; pass 0 to opt out explicitly.

ref.description: Filter results to repositories with a specific reference tag

tools.name: gortex__get_processes
tools.description: Returns discovered execution flows — named chains of function calls starting from entry points. Without id: list all processes. With id: full step-by-step call chain for that process.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

id.description: Optional process ID (e.g. process-0). When set, returns the full step-by-step call chain for that process instead of the list.

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.name: gortex__get_recent_changes
tools.description: Returns files and symbols that changed since the last call (watch mode only). Use to re-orient after the user edits files outside of Claude Code's view, without re-reading anything.

since.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (omit for all changes since index)

tools.name: gortex__get_repo_outline
tools.description: Narrative single-call overview of the indexed codebase: primary languages, top communities, load-bearing hotspots, most-imported files, and entry points. Use at session start (or when onboarding to an unfamiliar repo) instead of assembling this from graph_stats + analyze + manual inspection. Output stays under ~1k tokens.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.name: gortex__get_symbol
tools.description: Use instead of Read to locate a function, type, interface, or variable definition. Returns location and signature without reading the whole file.

project.description: Filter results to repositories in a specific project

ref.description: Filter results to repositories with a specific reference tag

repo.description: Filter results to a specific repository prefix

tools.name: gortex__get_symbol_history
tools.description: Returns symbols modified during the current session with modification counts. Flags churning symbols (modified 3+ times).

tools.name: gortex__get_symbol_source
tools.description: Returns the source code of a specific symbol (function, method, type) without reading the entire file. Use instead of Read when you know which symbol you need — saves 70-80% of tokens compared to reading the whole file.

compress_bodies.description: Replace function/method bodies in the returned source with a `{ /* N lines elided */ }` stub. Signatures, doc-comments, and structure stay intact. ~30-40% of original tokens. Useful when you only need the surface signature of the symbol, not its implementation. Default: false.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

if_none_match.description: ETag from a previous response — returns not_modified if content unchanged

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

tools.description: Given changed symbol IDs, traces the call graph to find test files and test functions that exercise those symbols. Use after editing to know exactly which tests to run — no guessing, no running the entire suite.

tools.name: gortex__get_untested_symbols
tools.description: Returns functions and methods in non-test files that no test file reaches via the call graph — the inverse of get_test_targets. Ranked by fan_in descending so the most-used untested symbols surface first. Use to prioritize where to add test coverage.

file_prefix.description: Restrict to symbols whose file path starts with this prefix (e.g. 'internal/auth/')

min_fan_in.description: Only flag symbols with at least this many callers; filters trivial helpers (default: 0)

tools.name: gortex__graph_stats
tools.description: Returns a compact summary of the indexed codebase: node/edge counts by kind and language. Call at session start to orient Claude in an unfamiliar repo.

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.name: gortex__index_repository
tools.description: Index or re-index a local repository path into Gortex. Call once at session start if not already running with --watch.

tools.name: gortex__list_repos
tools.description: Lists every project in the active workspace. Workspace-scope tool: do not pass `repo`. In workspace mode returns the auto-discovered, non-excluded children. In single-project mode returns the one bound project as a degenerate one-member workspace.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_delete
tools.description: Remove a single overlay file from the calling MCP session's overlay. The next tools/call will see the saved-buffer view for that path.

path.description: Repo-relative or absolute path of the overlay to remove.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_drop
tools.description: Tear down the calling MCP session's overlay entirely. Equivalent to calling overlay_delete on every attached path. The session remains live; a subsequent overlay_register / overlay_push starts a fresh overlay.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_keepalive
tools.description: Refresh the calling MCP session's overlay idle timer without changing any overlay content. Cheaper than re-pushing buffer content when the editor needs to extend the lease (e.g. the user is debugging or paused on a breakpoint and won't push for a while). Returns the resulting expires_at / idle_seconds so the editor can schedule the next keepalive. The MCP session disconnect path already drops the overlay synchronously, so keepalive is only needed for genuine idle gaps below the disconnect-detection threshold.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_list
tools.description: List every overlay file currently attached to the calling MCP session. Returns workspace_id, the count of files, and each overlay's path / content length / deleted flag / base_sha. The path and metadata are returned; content bytes are not, to keep the response small.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_push
tools.description: Push (or update) a single file overlay onto the current MCP session's overlay. The file at `path` is treated as if it contained `content` for the duration of every subsequent tools/call. Set `deleted: true` to mark a tombstone (queries see the file as missing). Drift detection: when `base_sha` is set, the daemon compares it to the on-disk git blob SHA at apply time and returns an overlay-drift error if they disagree.

base_sha.description: Git blob SHA the editor opened the file at. Empty disables drift detection.

content.description: Editor-buffer content. Empty + deleted=false is allowed and means "an empty file".

deleted.description: Mark the path as deleted (queries see no file).

tools.name: gortex__overlay_register
tools.description: Register an editor-overlay session bound to the current MCP session. Subsequent overlay_push calls attach in-flight editor buffers; every tools/call from this session then sees the overlay merged on top of the saved-buffer graph view. Idempotent — calling twice with the same workspace is a no-op.

workspace_id.description: Workspace slug the overlay belongs to. Optional; defaults to the session's bound workspace.

tools.name: gortex__plan_turn
tools.description: Opening-move router. Returns a short ranked list of recommended tool calls (with pre-filled args) for a task — 'what should I call first?'. Use before smart_context when you want a cheaper routing decision; call smart_context directly when you're committed to exploring.

tools.name: gortex__prefetch_context
tools.description: Predicts what context you will need next based on recent activity and a task description. Returns ranked symbols with relevance reasons.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous `next_cursor` to fetch the next page.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to keep on each candidate (e.g. "id,confidence,reason").

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

include_source.description: Include source code for top 5 candidates

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

max_tokens.description: Cap the marshaled response at approximately this many tokens (3.5 bytes/token heuristic; composable with max_bytes — tighter wins). Use when you have a token budget instead of a byte budget. Omit for no cap; pass 0 to opt out explicitly.

paginate.description: When true, the server caps each page at the project default budget and returns `next_cursor` for any tail.

recent_symbols.description: Comma-separated list of recently viewed symbol IDs

tools.name: gortex__preview_edit
tools.description: Speculatively apply one WorkspaceEdit (LSP-shaped: `changes` or `documentChanges`) to a fresh shadow view on top of the base graph, then return the impact — touched files, before/after symbol surfaces, broken callers, broken interface implementors, suggested test targets, and (when the LSP server for the language is running) the diagnostics that the change would produce. Disk is never written; the base graph is never mutated. The shadow view is built per call and discarded with the response. Useful when you want to know if a refactor is safe before staging it.

diagnostics.description: Run LSP diagnostics on every touched file in the simulated view and include them in the response. Default true. Set to false to skip the LSP round-trip when you only care about the graph delta.

diagnostics_timeout_ms.description: How long to wait for the LSP server to publish diagnostics after the simulated didChange (default 1500ms). Tighten when latency matters; loosen for cold language servers.

inherit_overlay.description: When true, apply the edit on top of the caller's current overlay (if any) instead of pristine base. Default false. Use this to preview an edit on top of unsaved editor buffers.

workspace_edit.description: LSP WorkspaceEdit as a JSON string. Either `{"changes": {uri: [TextEdit, ...], ...}}` or `{"documentChanges": [{"textDocument": {"uri": "..."}, "edits": [TextEdit, ...]}, ...]}`. TextEdit is `{"range": {"start": {"line": N, "character": M}, "end": {...}}, "newText": "..."}` with 0-indexed line/char. URIs can be `file://`, absolute paths, or repo-relative paths.

tools.name: gortex__query_memories
tools.description: Search the cross-session memory store by symbol, file, tag, kind, source, author, free-text, or recency. Returns matches sorted pinned-first, then importance-DESC, then UpdatedAt-DESC. Unlike query_notes, memories are workspace-wide and have no session boundary.

file_path.description: Return memories anchored to this file path.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx, or toon

kind.description: Filter by memory kind: invariant | constraint | convention | gotcha | decision | incident | reference.

min_importance.description: Only return memories with importance >= this value (1..5).

since.description: Only return memories updated at or after this RFC-3339 timestamp.

source.description: Filter by memory source: manual | distilled | incident | review.

symbol_id.description: Return memories anchored or auto-linked to this symbol.

text.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on body or title.

tools.name: gortex__query_notes
tools.description: Search saved notes for the current session / workspace. Filter by symbol, file, tag, free-text body match, session, or recency.

file_path.description: Return notes attached to this file path

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx, or toon

session_id.description: Limit to a specific session. Defaults to the current session; pass 'all' to query every session.

since.description: Only return notes updated at or after this RFC-3339 timestamp

symbol_id.description: Return notes attached or auto-linked to this symbol

text.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on the body

tools.name: gortex__read_file
tools.description: Reads a whole file by path and returns its content. Use sparingly — prefer get_symbol_source / get_editing_context for code. Useful when you need a non-indexed file (config, fixture, raw markdown) or when you genuinely need the full body. With compress_bodies=true, every function/method body is replaced by a `{ /* N lines elided */ }` stub — signatures + structure preserved, ~30-40% of original tokens. Composable with format:"gcx".

compress_bodies.description: Replace function/method bodies with elided stubs (default: false)

if_none_match.description: ETag from a previous response — returns not_modified if content unchanged

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation flag rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

path.description: Absolute path, or repo-prefixed / repo-root-relative path

tools.name: gortex__rename_symbol
tools.description: Generates coordinated multi-file edit instructions for renaming a symbol. Returns {file, line, old_text, new_text, confidence} for every reference. Use dry_run to preview, then apply edits with the Edit tool.

tools.name: gortex__save_note
tools.description: Persist an agent-authored note for the current session. Optionally attach to a symbol or file; the body is scanned for symbol references and auto-linked. Use to record decisions, gotchas, follow-ups, or context the agent will need after a compaction. Pass `id` to update an existing note.

body.description: Free-form note text. Symbol IDs (file/path.go::Name) and bare identifier names are auto-linked when they resolve in the graph.

file_path.description: Primary file the note attaches to (optional)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx, or toon

id.description: Existing note ID — passing it switches the call from create to update.

links.description: Comma-separated symbol IDs to attach explicitly (merged with auto-detected links)

no_autolink.description: Skip the body→symbol auto-linker (set true when the body intentionally mentions identifiers that should not be linked).

pinned.description: Pinned notes are always returned by distill_session and never evicted by the store cap.

symbol_id.description: Primary symbol the note attaches to (optional)

tags.description: Comma-separated labels — e.g. 'decision,bug,follow-up'. Notes tagged 'decision' are surfaced separately in distill_session.

assistantTexts:
Found and fixed a bug in `udd-web/lib/ai/service.ts` at line 357.

**The bug:** `persistFiles` used `if (!result.files.length) return;` without checking if `result.files` is defined. If the AI generator ever returns a result with no `files` property (e.g., an error result or malformed response), this throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`, crashing the task instead of failing gracefully.

**The fix:** Changed to `if (!result.files?.length) return;` — optional chaining safely handles undefined `files` by returning early, which is the intended no-op behavior for empty file sets.

**Why it matters:** This is in the critical path of every AI task completion. A single malformed generator response would leave the task hanging or unhandled instead of cleanly completing with no files.

content.text:
/focus is not a recognized command. Here's what I can help with:

**Session controls:**
- `/reasoning` — toggle thinking mode on/off
- `/status` or `📊` — show session status card

**Available skills** (read with `read <path>`):
- `gortex-guide` — code intelligence tools
- `gortex-explore` — explore unfamiliar code
- `gortex-debug` — trace errors
- `gortex-impact` — safety analysis before changes
- `gortex-refactor` — rename, extract, restructure
- `diagram-maker` — create diagrams
- `spike` — feasibility prototypes
- `taskflow` — durable multi-step jobs
- And more — see full list in context

**What do you want to do?**

workspace: udd-web
Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /Users/asherlewis/PROJECTS/udd-web/lib/ai/service.ts.

Found and fixed a bug in `udd-web/lib/ai/service.ts` at line 357.

**The bug:** `persistFiles` used `if (!result.files.length) return;` without checking if `result.files` is defined. If the AI generator ever returns a result with no `files` property (e.g., an error result or malformed response), this throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`, crashing the task instead of failing gracefully.

**The fix:** Changed to `if (!result.files?.length) return;` — optional chaining safely handles undefined `files` by returning early, which is the intended no-op behavior for empty file sets.

**Why it matters:** This is in the critical path of every AI task completion. A single malformed generator response would leave the task hanging or unhandled instead of cleanly completing with no files.

tools.name: edit
tools.description: Edit a single file using exact text replacement. Every edits[].oldText must match a unique, non-overlapping region of the original file. If two changes affect the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead of emitting overlapping edits. Do not include large unchanged regions just to connect distant changes.

path.description: Path to the file to edit (relative or absolute)

edits.description: One or more targeted replacements. Each edit is matched against the original file, not incrementally. Do not include overlapping or nested edits. If two changes touch the same block or nearby lines, merge them into one edit instead.

tools.name: exec
tools.description: Execute shell commands with background continuation for work that starts now. Use yieldMs/background to continue later via process tool. For long-running work started now, rely on automatic completion wake when it is enabled and the command emits output or fails; otherwise use process to confirm completion. Use process whenever you need logs, status, input, or intervention. Use pty=true for TTY-required commands (terminal UIs, coding agents).

timeout.description: Timeout in seconds (optional, kills process on expiry)

pty.description: Run in a pseudo-terminal (PTY) when available (TTY-required CLIs, coding agents)

elevated.description: Run on the host with elevated permissions (if allowed)

security.description: Ignored for normal calls; exec security is set by tools.exec.security and host approvals.

tools.name: gortex__analyze
tools.description: Unified graph analysis. kind=dead_code: symbols with zero incoming edges. kind=hotspots: high-complexity symbols by fan-in/out. kind=cycles: circular dependency chains. kind=would_create_cycle: check if a new edge would form a cycle (requires from_id, to_id). kind=todos: list KindTodo nodes with optional tag/assignee/ticket/has_assignee filters. kind=blame: run `git blame` against the indexed repo and stamp meta.last_authored on every symbol-level node. kind=coverage: parse a Go cover.out profile (path via `profile` arg) and stamp meta.coverage_pct on every executable symbol. kind=stale_code: list symbols whose meta.last_authored is older than the threshold (requires blame-enriched graph). kind=ownership: group blame metadata by author email — symbol count, files touched, oldest/newest timestamps; supports path_prefix scoping (requires blame-enriched graph). kind=coverage_gaps: list symbols whose meta.coverage_pct falls in [min_pct, max_pct) — sorted ascending so the most undertested code surfaces first (requires coverage-enriched graph). kind=unsafe_patterns: bundled scan for panic-prone / undefined-behaviour primitives across every supported language — Go panic, Rust .unwrap/.expect/panic!/todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/assert!/unsafe blocks, Python assert, JS/TS throw — aggregated into one row-per-site response with a per-detector summary. Filters: language, detector, severity, path_prefix, limit, exclude_tests. kind=health_score: composite per-symbol health value (0..100) + A..F grade aggregated from coverage_pct, complexity (fan-in/out + community-crossings), recency (last_authored), and session churn. Per-axis breakdown surfaced on every row; missing axes are skipped (not zero-imputed). Always returns a population distribution (mean / median / std_dev / Gini coefficient over inequality of risk + per-grade counts). Pass roll_up='file' or 'repo' for per-file / per-repo averages with min/max bands and per-grade counts. Filters: path_prefix, kinds, grade, min_score, max_score, min_axes, limit, roll_up. Sorted ascending so worst symbols surface first.

base_kind.description: (cross_repo) Scope to one base relation — calls, implements, or extends

detector.description: (unsafe_patterns) Comma-separated subset of bundled detector names — panic-in-library, unsafe-rust-unwrap, unsafe-rust-panic-macro, unsafe-rust-assert-macro, unsafe-rust-block, unsafe-python-assert, unsafe-js-throw

email.description: (stale_code) Filter to a single author email

exclude_tests.description: (unsafe_patterns) Override the per-detector default (defaults to true — test-only matches are dropped)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format, per-kind hand-tuned encoder), or toon

framework.description: (dbt_models) Filter to one transformation framework — dbt or sqlmesh

grade.description: (health_score) Comma-separated A..F subset to keep — e.g. 'd,f' for the worst-scoring symbols only

has_assignee.description: (todos) Keep only TODOs that have an assignee set

include_variables.description: (dead_code) Include variable nodes (default false — usually false positives without data-flow analysis)

kind.description: Analysis kind: dead_code | hotspots | cycles | would_create_cycle | todos | blame | coverage | stale_code | ownership | coverage_gaps | stale_flags | releases | cgo_users | wasm_users | orphan_tables | unreferenced_tables | coverage_summary | channel_ops | goroutine_spawns | field_writers | race_writes | unclosed_channels | unsafe_patterns | health_score | annotation_users | config_readers | event_emitters | pubsub | string_emitters | error_surface | log_events | sql_rebuild | external_calls | routes | models | components | k8s_resources | images | kustomize | cross_repo | dbt_models

kinds.description: (stale_code, ownership) Comma-separated kinds — default function,method; pass 'all' for every blame-eligible kind

language.description: (unsafe_patterns) Comma-separated subset of languages to keep — rust, python, javascript, typescript, go

limit.description: (cross_repo, error_surface, unsafe_patterns) Cap the number of rows returned — default 200

materialized.description: (dbt_models) Substring match on the model materialization — table, view, incremental, …

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

max_pct.description: (coverage_gaps) Upper-exclusive coverage threshold — default 100, i.e. anything not fully covered

max_score.description: (health_score) Drop rows whose composite score is above this (0..100)

min_axes.description: (health_score) Require at least this many populated axes per row (default 1; raise to demand multi-signal confidence)

min_score.description: (health_score) Drop rows whose composite score is below this (0..100)

min_symbols.description: (ownership) Drop authors with fewer than this many symbols — default 1

older_than.description: (stale_code) Symbols last touched more than this many days ago — default 365

path_prefix.description: (ownership, coverage_gaps) Scope to nodes under this file-path prefix — e.g. 'internal/auth/'

profile.description: (coverage) Path to a Go cover.out profile, absolute or relative to the indexed repo root

provider.description: (stale_flags) Filter to a single provider — launchdarkly, growthbook, unleash, internal

repo.description: (cross_repo) Scope to repo-boundary dependencies touching this repository prefix on either side

roll_up.description: (health_score) Aggregate per-symbol scores up to a coarser scope — 'file' (per-file average + per-grade counts) or 'repo' (per-repo). Omit for per-symbol rows.

scope.description: (cycles) File path or package prefix to limit scope

severity.description: (unsafe_patterns) Comma-separated subset of severity labels to keep — error, warning, info

tag.description: (todos) Filter by tag — TODO / FIXME / HACK / XXX / NOTE — case-insensitive

ticket.description: (todos) Filter by exact ticket reference — e.g. PROJ-42

tools.name: gortex__apply_code_action
tools.description: Applies a single LSP code action to disk. Pass the action's `index` from a previous get_code_actions call, plus the same `path`/`start_line`/`only` tuple to re-resolve the action list.

index.description: Index into the action list returned by get_code_actions

repo.description: Filter to a specific repository prefix

tools.name: gortex__audit_agent_config
tools.description: Scans agent config files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .cursor/rules, .github/copilot-instructions.md, etc.) for stale symbol references, dead file paths, and bloat — validated against the Gortex graph.

files.description: Optional comma-separated file paths to audit (relative to repo root). If omitted, auto-discovers known agent config files.

root.description: Optional repo root override. Defaults to the indexer's root.

tools.name: gortex__batch_edit
tools.description: Applies multiple symbol edits in dependency order. Re-indexes after each edit. Stops on failure and reports status.

dry_run.description: Return dependency-ordered plan without applying changes

edits.description: JSON array of {id, old_source, new_source} objects

tools.name: gortex__batch_symbols
tools.description: Returns signatures, source code, callers, and callees for multiple symbols in one call. Use instead of calling get_symbol_source or get_symbol multiple times — saves 60% round-trip overhead.

context_lines.description: Extra lines above/below source (default: 3, only if include_source)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

ids.description: Comma-separated list of symbol IDs

if_none_match.description: ETag from a previous response — returns not_modified if content unchanged

include_source.description: Include source code for each symbol (default: false)

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

tools.name: gortex__check_guards
tools.description: Evaluates project-specific guard rules against a set of changed symbols. Reports co-change and boundary violations.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

ids.description: Comma-separated list of changed symbol IDs

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

tools.name: gortex__compare_with_overlay
tools.description: Run a graph query against both the base (saved-buffer) graph and the calling session's overlay (editor-buffer) view, then report the delta. The base side answers "what's true now?"; the overlay side answers "what would be true if I committed this buffer?". Useful for previewing the impact of an unsaved edit on callers, dependents, or call chains. Supported `kind` values: find_usages, get_callers, get_call_chain, get_dependencies, get_dependents.

depth.description: Traversal depth for chain / dependency queries (default 2).

kind.description: Query kind to run. One of: find_usages, get_callers, get_call_chain, get_dependencies, get_dependents.

limit.description: Maximum number of nodes to return per side (default 50).

tools.name: gortex__contracts
tools.description:
API contracts tool. action=list (default): lists detected contracts (HTTP, gRPC, GraphQL, topics, WebSocket, env, OpenAPI). action=check: detects orphan providers/consumers across repos. action=validate: diffs provider↔consumer request/response shapes and flags breaking/warning/info issues.

DEFAULT SCOPE for list: auto-scopes to the active project's repos and hides dependency-origin contracts (type=dependency, vendored paths like vendor/, node_modules/). The response reports other_repos (count of contracts filtered out of scope) and dependencies_skipped (count of dep contracts hidden). To widen scope, pass repo=<prefix>, project=<name>, ref=<tag>, or all_repos=true. To include dependency contracts, pass include_deps=true.

all_repos.description: (list) Disable active-project auto-scope; return contracts from every indexed repo. Default false.

cursor.description: (list) Opaque pagination cursor from a previous `next_cursor` to fetch the next page.

fields.description: (list) Comma-separated list of fields to keep on each contract (e.g. "type,role,id").

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

include_deps.description: (list) Include type=dependency contracts and contracts from vendored paths (vendor/, node_modules/, Pods/, .venv/). Default false.

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

paginate.description: (list) When true, caps each page at the project default budget and returns `next_cursor` for any tail.

project.description: Filter to repositories in a specific project (resolves to the project's repo set)

ref.description: Filter to repositories tagged with this ref

repo.description: Filter by repository prefix

role.description: (list) Filter by role: provider or consumer

type.description: (list) Filter by type: http, grpc, graphql, topic, ws, env, openapi, dependency

tools.name: gortex__detect_changes
tools.description: Maps uncommitted git changes to symbols in the graph and runs blast radius analysis. The key pre-commit review tool.

tools.name: gortex__diff_context
tools.description: Returns graph-enriched context for symbols affected by a git diff: source, callers, callees, community, processes, and per-file risk.

tools.name: gortex__distill_session
tools.description: Aggregate a session's saved notes into a digest: top symbols / files / tags, pinned notes, recent excerpts, and a short summary. Use after a context compaction to recover what the agent was doing.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx, or toon

max_recent.description: Number of recent note excerpts to include (default: 8)

session_id.description: Session to distill. Defaults to the current session; pass 'all' to distill every session for the current workspace.

tools.name: gortex__edit_file
tools.description: Edit any file (markdown, config, spec, template, source) by exact string replacement — no Read needed. Accepts absolute paths or paths relative to the indexed repo root. Writes atomically (temp+rename) and re-indexes the file so the graph stays fresh. Pass dry_run=true to validate the replacement without writing. Complements edit_symbol for non-code files that have no symbol ID.

dry_run.description: Validate the replacement and report what would change without writing (default: false)

old_string.description: Exact text to replace (must be unique unless replace_all=true)

path.description: Absolute path, or repo-prefixed / repo-root-relative path

replace_all.description: Replace every occurrence instead of requiring uniqueness (default: false)

tools.name: gortex__edit_symbol
tools.description: Edit a symbol's source code directly by ID — no Read needed. Gortex resolves the file and line range, finds the old_source fragment, replaces it with new_source, and writes the file. Eliminates the Read→Edit roundtrip for ~80% of edits.

old_source.description: Exact source fragment to replace (must be unique within the symbol)

tools.name: gortex__explain_change_impact
tools.description: Given a list of symbols you plan to modify, returns risk-tiered blast radius: d=1 will break, d=2 likely affected, d=3 needs testing. Includes affected processes and communities.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

ids.description: Comma-separated list of symbol IDs to modify

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

tools.name: gortex__export_context
tools.description: Generates a portable context briefing for a task as self-contained markdown or JSON. Use for sharing context outside MCP — paste into Slack, PRs, docs, or non-MCP AI tools.

entry_point.description: Optional symbol ID or file path to start from

format.description: Output format: markdown (default) or json

token_budget.description: Approximate token budget for output (default: 2000, max: 8000)

tools.name: gortex__feedback
tools.description: Agent learning feedback. action=record: report which symbols from smart_context/prefetch_context were useful, not_needed, or missing (improves future context). action=query: aggregated stats — most useful, most missed, accuracy.

format.description: (query) Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

missing.description: (record) Comma-separated symbol IDs that should have been included

not_needed.description: (record) Comma-separated symbol IDs that were returned but not needed

task.description: (record) The task description used in the original context call

tool_source.description: Which tool produced the context: smart_context or prefetch_context (default: smart_context). For query: filter by source or 'all'

top_n.description: (query) Number of top symbols to return per category (default: 10)

useful.description: (record) Comma-separated symbol IDs that were useful

tools.name: gortex__find_clones
tools.description: Surfaces near-duplicate ('clone') function/method clusters from the EdgeSimilarTo graph layer. Each cluster is a connected component of bodies whose MinHash + LSH estimated Jaccard similarity crossed the index-time threshold — catches copy-paste and renamed-variable (Type-1/Type-2) clones. Every member is flagged is_dead (zero incoming calls/refs), so dead_only=true yields the Gortex-unique "dead duplicates of live code" diagnostic: dead functions that are near-copies of code still in use.

dead_only.description: Only return clusters that contain at least one dead-code symbol — the "dead duplicates of live code" view. Default false.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

limit.description: Maximum clusters to return (default: 50). Clusters are ranked largest-first.

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

min_similarity.description: Only report clone pairs at or above this estimated Jaccard similarity (0..1). Default 0 — every recorded EdgeSimilarTo edge.

path_prefix.description: Restrict to symbols whose file path starts with this prefix.

repo.description: Restrict to symbols in a specific repository (RepoPrefix exact match).

tools.name: gortex__find_implementations
tools.description: Finds all concrete types that implement an interface. Use before changing an interface to identify all types that will be affected.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

min_tier.description: Filter edges by minimum confidence tier. Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__find_import_path
tools.description: Given a symbol name you want to use in a file, returns the correct import path. Use instead of reading files or guessing package paths.

name.description: Name of the symbol to import

path.description: File where you want to use the symbol (relative path)

tools.name: gortex__find_overrides
tools.description: Finds all methods that override the given method (children) or the parent methods it overrides. Backed by EdgeOverrides materialised at index time and promoted to lsp_dispatch when an LSP is available.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

min_tier.description: Filter edges by minimum confidence tier. Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__find_usages
tools.description: Use instead of Grep to find every reference to a symbol across the codebase. Returns precise locations with zero false positives.

exclude_tests.description: Drop references originating in test functions (set true to see only production usages)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

max_tokens.description: Cap the marshaled response at approximately this many tokens (3.5 bytes/token heuristic; composable with max_bytes — tighter wins). Use when you have a token budget instead of a byte budget. Omit for no cap; pass 0 to opt out explicitly.

min_tier.description: Filter edges by minimum confidence tier. Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

project.description: Filter results to repositories in a specific project

ref.description: Filter results to repositories with a specific reference tag

repo.description: Filter results to a specific repository prefix

tools.name: gortex__fix_all_in_file
tools.description: Loops codeAction → apply → re-collect-diagnostics until convergence. Defaults to {quickfix, source.organizeImports}; pass `kinds` to override. Returns iteration count, total actions applied, files touched, and final diagnostics.

diagnostic_timeout_ms.description: How long to wait for refreshed diagnostics each iteration (default: 5000)

max_actions_per_iter.description: Cap on actions applied per iteration (default: 50)

repo.description: Filter to a specific repository prefix

tools.name: gortex__flow_between
tools.description: Returns ranked dataflow paths between two symbols. Walks EdgeValueFlow / EdgeArgOf / EdgeReturnsTo forward from source to sink — the CPG-lite primitive that answers "where does this value flow?". Pairs with taint_paths for pattern-driven sweeps. Every EdgeStep carries an origin tier (lsp_resolved / ast_resolved / …) and a coarse `tier` label (lsp / ast / heuristic); pass `min_tier` to prune edges below a threshold during traversal.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

max_paths.description: Maximum number of paths to return (default: 10)

min_tier.description: Minimum per-edge Origin tier to traverse. Accepts one of: lsp_resolved, lsp_dispatch, ast_resolved, ast_inferred, text_matched. Empty (default) disables the filter.

source_id.description: Source symbol node ID — typically a function, method, or parameter

tools.description: Return the current active project name and its list of member repositories.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__get_callers
tools.description: Returns all callers of a function without reading source. Use instead of Grep when you need to know who calls a function.

exclude_tests.description: Drop callers originating in test functions (set true when you want production callers only)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

min_tier.description: Filter edges by minimum confidence tier. Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__get_class_hierarchy
tools.description: Returns the inheritance subgraph around a type, interface, or method. Walks EdgeExtends + EdgeImplements + EdgeComposes for type nodes and EdgeOverrides for method nodes — the same graph data find_implementations and find_overrides expose, but as a multi-hop tree so an agent gets the whole chain (parents → root, children → leaves) in one call. Use before refactoring an OO hierarchy or to answer 'what does this class inherit from / who subclasses it'.

depth.description: Max hops to walk in each direction (default: 5, hard cap: 64)

direction.description: 'up' (parents/interfaces this extends or implements; methods this overrides), 'down' (subtypes / implementers / overriders), or 'both' (default)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

id.description: Seed node ID — a type, interface, or method

include_methods.description: When true and the seed/visited node is a type or interface, also include its methods (via EdgeMemberOf) and walk the EdgeOverrides chain rooted at each method.

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

min_tier.description: Filter edges by minimum confidence tier. Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__get_cluster
tools.description: Returns the immediate neighbourhood around a node — all symbols it touches and that touch it. Useful for understanding a module's coupling before refactoring.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

repo.description: Filter to a specific repository prefix

id.description: Optional community ID (e.g. community-0). When set, returns full details of that community instead of the list.

tools.name: gortex__get_dependencies
tools.description: Returns what a symbol or file depends on — imports, calls, type references — without reading any files. Use before editing to understand incoming contracts.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__get_dependents

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

Values (highest to lowest): lsp_resolved (compiler-verified), lsp_dispatch (interface→impl via semantic provider), ast_resolved (tree-sitter direct match), ast_inferred (type heuristic), text_matched (name-only). Omit for no filter. Use lsp_resolved for high-stakes refactors where false positives are expensive.

tools.name: gortex__get_diagnostics
tools.description: Returns the most recent LSP diagnostics for a file. The file is auto-opened on the server if it isn't already, and the call optionally waits for the next publishDiagnostics burst.

project.description: Filter to repositories in a specific project

repo.description: Filter to a specific repository prefix

timeout_ms.description: Wait timeout in milliseconds when wait=true (default: 5000)

wait.description: Block for the next publishDiagnostics (default: false)

tools.name: gortex__get_edit_plan
tools.description: Given symbols you plan to change, returns a dependency-ordered list of files and symbols to edit — definitions first, then implementations, then callers, then tests. Eliminates manual dependency reasoning. Use before any multi-file refactor.

ids.description: Comma-separated list of symbol IDs to change

tools.name: gortex__get_editing_context
tools.description: The primary tool to call before editing any file. Returns all symbols defined in the file, their signatures, direct dependencies, and immediate callers — everything needed to code without reading raw source lines.

compress_bodies.description: Also return a source_compressed view of the whole file with every function/method body replaced by a `{ /* N lines elided */ }` stub. Signatures, imports, types, and comments are preserved verbatim. Roughly 60-70% fewer tokens than raw source. Composable with format:"gcx". Default: false.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

if_none_match.description: ETag from a previous response — returns not_modified if content unchanged

max_tokens.description: Cap the marshaled response at approximately this many tokens (3.5 bytes/token heuristic; composable with max_bytes — tighter wins). Use when you have a token budget instead of a byte budget. Omit for no cap; pass 0 to opt out explicitly.

ref.description: Filter results to repositories with a specific reference tag

tools.name: gortex__get_processes
tools.description: Returns discovered execution flows — named chains of function calls starting from entry points. Without id: list all processes. With id: full step-by-step call chain for that process.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

id.description: Optional process ID (e.g. process-0). When set, returns the full step-by-step call chain for that process instead of the list.

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.name: gortex__get_recent_changes
tools.description: Returns files and symbols that changed since the last call (watch mode only). Use to re-orient after the user edits files outside of Claude Code's view, without re-reading anything.

since.description: ISO 8601 timestamp (omit for all changes since index)

tools.name: gortex__get_repo_outline
tools.description: Narrative single-call overview of the indexed codebase: primary languages, top communities, load-bearing hotspots, most-imported files, and entry points. Use at session start (or when onboarding to an unfamiliar repo) instead of assembling this from graph_stats + analyze + manual inspection. Output stays under ~1k tokens.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.name: gortex__get_symbol
tools.description: Use instead of Read to locate a function, type, interface, or variable definition. Returns location and signature without reading the whole file.

project.description: Filter results to repositories in a specific project

ref.description: Filter results to repositories with a specific reference tag

repo.description: Filter results to a specific repository prefix

tools.name: gortex__get_symbol_history
tools.description: Returns symbols modified during the current session with modification counts. Flags churning symbols (modified 3+ times).

tools.name: gortex__get_symbol_source
tools.description: Returns the source code of a specific symbol (function, method, type) without reading the entire file. Use instead of Read when you know which symbol you need — saves 70-80% of tokens compared to reading the whole file.

compress_bodies.description: Replace function/method bodies in the returned source with a `{ /* N lines elided */ }` stub. Signatures, doc-comments, and structure stay intact. ~30-40% of original tokens. Useful when you only need the surface signature of the symbol, not its implementation. Default: false.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

if_none_match.description: ETag from a previous response — returns not_modified if content unchanged

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

tools.description: Given changed symbol IDs, traces the call graph to find test files and test functions that exercise those symbols. Use after editing to know exactly which tests to run — no guessing, no running the entire suite.

ids.description: Comma-separated list of changed symbol IDs

tools.name: gortex__get_untested_symbols
tools.description: Returns functions and methods in non-test files that no test file reaches via the call graph — the inverse of get_test_targets. Ranked by fan_in descending so the most-used untested symbols surface first. Use to prioritize where to add test coverage.

file_prefix.description: Restrict to symbols whose file path starts with this prefix (e.g. 'internal/auth/')

min_fan_in.description: Only flag symbols with at least this many callers; filters trivial helpers (default: 0)

tools.name: gortex__graph_stats
tools.description: Returns a compact summary of the indexed codebase: node/edge counts by kind and language. Call at session start to orient Claude in an unfamiliar repo.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.description: Reports the health and completeness of the Gortex index: parse failures, stale files, language coverage, and health score.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.name: gortex__index_repository
tools.description: Index or re-index a local repository path into Gortex. Call once at session start if not already running with --watch.

tools.name: gortex__list_repos
tools.description: Lists every project in the active workspace. Workspace-scope tool: do not pass `repo`. In workspace mode returns the auto-discovered, non-excluded children. In single-project mode returns the one bound project as a degenerate one-member workspace.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation metadata rides on the response.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_delete
tools.description: Remove a single overlay file from the calling MCP session's overlay. The next tools/call will see the saved-buffer view for that path.

path.description: Repo-relative or absolute path of the overlay to remove.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_drop
tools.description: Tear down the calling MCP session's overlay entirely. Equivalent to calling overlay_delete on every attached path. The session remains live; a subsequent overlay_register / overlay_push starts a fresh overlay.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_keepalive
tools.description: Refresh the calling MCP session's overlay idle timer without changing any overlay content. Cheaper than re-pushing buffer content when the editor needs to extend the lease (e.g. the user is debugging or paused on a breakpoint and won't push for a while). Returns the resulting expires_at / idle_seconds so the editor can schedule the next keepalive. The MCP session disconnect path already drops the overlay synchronously, so keepalive is only needed for genuine idle gaps below the disconnect-detection threshold.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_list
tools.description: List every overlay file currently attached to the calling MCP session. Returns workspace_id, the count of files, and each overlay's path / content length / deleted flag / base_sha. The path and metadata are returned; content bytes are not, to keep the response small.

tools.name: gortex__overlay_push
tools.description: Push (or update) a single file overlay onto the current MCP session's overlay. The file at `path` is treated as if it contained `content` for the duration of every subsequent tools/call. Set `deleted: true` to mark a tombstone (queries see the file as missing). Drift detection: when `base_sha` is set, the daemon compares it to the on-disk git blob SHA at apply time and returns an overlay-drift error if they disagree.

base_sha.description: Git blob SHA the editor opened the file at. Empty disables drift detection.

content.description: Editor-buffer content. Empty + deleted=false is allowed and means "an empty file".

deleted.description: Mark the path as deleted (queries see no file).

tools.name: gortex__overlay_register
tools.description: Register an editor-overlay session bound to the current MCP session. Subsequent overlay_push calls attach in-flight editor buffers; every tools/call from this session then sees the overlay merged on top of the saved-buffer graph view. Idempotent — calling twice with the same workspace is a no-op.

workspace_id.description: Workspace slug the overlay belongs to. Optional; defaults to the session's bound workspace.

tools.name: gortex__plan_turn
tools.description: Opening-move router. Returns a short ranked list of recommended tool calls (with pre-filled args) for a task — 'what should I call first?'. Use before smart_context when you want a cheaper routing decision; call smart_context directly when you're committed to exploring.

tools.name: gortex__prefetch_context
tools.description: Predicts what context you will need next based on recent activity and a task description. Returns ranked symbols with relevance reasons.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor from a previous `next_cursor` to fetch the next page.

fields.description: Comma-separated list of fields to keep on each candidate (e.g. "id,confidence,reason").

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx (GCX1 compact wire format), or toon

include_source.description: Include source code for top 5 candidates

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes. The longest list is trimmed; truncation metadata rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

max_tokens.description: Cap the marshaled response at approximately this many tokens (3.5 bytes/token heuristic; composable with max_bytes — tighter wins). Use when you have a token budget instead of a byte budget. Omit for no cap; pass 0 to opt out explicitly.

paginate.description: When true, the server caps each page at the project default budget and returns `next_cursor` for any tail.

recent_symbols.description: Comma-separated list of recently viewed symbol IDs

tools.name: gortex__preview_edit
tools.description: Speculatively apply one WorkspaceEdit (LSP-shaped: `changes` or `documentChanges`) to a fresh shadow view on top of the base graph, then return the impact — touched files, before/after symbol surfaces, broken callers, broken interface implementors, suggested test targets, and (when the LSP server for the language is running) the diagnostics that the change would produce. Disk is never written; the base graph is never mutated. The shadow view is built per call and discarded with the response. Useful when you want to know if a refactor is safe before staging it.

diagnostics.description: Run LSP diagnostics on every touched file in the simulated view and include them in the response. Default true. Set to false to skip the LSP round-trip when you only care about the graph delta.

diagnostics_timeout_ms.description: How long to wait for the LSP server to publish diagnostics after the simulated didChange (default 1500ms). Tighten when latency matters; loosen for cold language servers.

inherit_overlay.description: When true, apply the edit on top of the caller's current overlay (if any) instead of pristine base. Default false. Use this to preview an edit on top of unsaved editor buffers.

workspace_edit.description: LSP WorkspaceEdit as a JSON string. Either `{"changes": {uri: [TextEdit, ...], ...}}` or `{"documentChanges": [{"textDocument": {"uri": "..."}, "edits": [TextEdit, ...]}, ...]}`. TextEdit is `{"range": {"start": {"line": N, "character": M}, "end": {...}}, "newText": "..."}` with 0-indexed line/char. URIs can be `file://`, absolute paths, or repo-relative paths.

tools.name: gortex__query_memories
tools.description: Search the cross-session memory store by symbol, file, tag, kind, source, author, free-text, or recency. Returns matches sorted pinned-first, then importance-DESC, then UpdatedAt-DESC. Unlike query_notes, memories are workspace-wide and have no session boundary.

file_path.description: Return memories anchored to this file path.

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx, or toon

kind.description: Filter by memory kind: invariant | constraint | convention | gotcha | decision | incident | reference.

min_importance.description: Only return memories with importance >= this value (1..5).

since.description: Only return memories updated at or after this RFC-3339 timestamp.

source.description: Filter by memory source: manual | distilled | incident | review.

symbol_id.description: Return memories anchored or auto-linked to this symbol.

text.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on body or title.

tools.name: gortex__query_notes
tools.description: Search saved notes for the current session / workspace. Filter by symbol, file, tag, free-text body match, session, or recency.

file_path.description: Return notes attached to this file path

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx, or toon

session_id.description: Limit to a specific session. Defaults to the current session; pass 'all' to query every session.

since.description: Only return notes updated at or after this RFC-3339 timestamp

symbol_id.description: Return notes attached or auto-linked to this symbol

text.description: Case-insensitive substring filter on the body

tools.name: gortex__read_file
tools.description: Reads a whole file by path and returns its content. Use sparingly — prefer get_symbol_source / get_editing_context for code. Useful when you need a non-indexed file (config, fixture, raw markdown) or when you genuinely need the full body. With compress_bodies=true, every function/method body is replaced by a `{ /* N lines elided */ }` stub — signatures + structure preserved, ~30-40% of original tokens. Composable with format:"gcx".

compress_bodies.description: Replace function/method bodies with elided stubs (default: false)

if_none_match.description: ETag from a previous response — returns not_modified if content unchanged

max_bytes.description: Cap the marshaled response at this many bytes; truncation flag rides on the response. Omit for no cap.

path.description: Absolute path, or repo-prefixed / repo-root-relative path

tools.name: gortex__rename_symbol
tools.description: Generates coordinated multi-file edit instructions for renaming a symbol. Returns {file, line, old_text, new_text, confidence} for every reference. Use dry_run to preview, then apply edits with the Edit tool.

tools.name: gortex__save_note
tools.description: Persist an agent-authored note for the current session. Optionally attach to a symbol or file; the body is scanned for symbol references and auto-linked. Use to record decisions, gotchas, follow-ups, or context the agent will need after a compaction. Pass `id` to update an existing note.

body.description: Free-form note text. Symbol IDs (file/path.go::Name) and bare identifier names are auto-linked when they resolve in the graph.

file_path.description: Primary file the note attaches to (optional)

format.description: Output format: json (default), gcx, or toon

id.description: Existing note ID — passing it switches the call from create to update.

links.description: Comma-separated symbol IDs to attach explicitly (merged with auto-detected links)

no_autolink.description: Skip the body→symbol auto-linker (set true when the body intentionally mentions identifiers that should not be linked).

pinned.description: Pinned notes are always returned by distill_session and never evicted by the store cap.

symbol_id.description: Primary symbol the note attaches to (optional)

tags.description: Comma-separated labels — e.g. 'decision,bug,follow-up'. Notes tagged 'decision' are surfaced separately in distill_session.

Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /Users/asherlewis/PROJECTS/udd-web/lib/ai/service.ts.

Found and fixed a bug in `udd-web/lib/ai/service.ts` at line 357.

**The bug:** `persistFiles` used `if (!result.files.length) return;` without checking if `result.files` is defined. If the AI generator ever returns a result with no `files` property (e.g., an error result or malformed response), this throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`, crashing the task instead of failing gracefully.

**The fix:** Changed to `if (!result.files?.length) return;` — optional chaining safely handles undefined `files` by returning early, which is the intended no-op behavior for empty file sets.

**Why it matters:** This is in the critical path of every AI task completion. A single malformed generator response would leave the task hanging or unhandled instead of cleanly completing with no files.

assistantTexts: Now let me look for another bug in the same file or nearby code:

Done. Fixed 2 bugs in `udd-web/lib/ai/service.ts`:

**Bug 1** — `persistFiles` (line 357)
- **Was:** `if (!result.files.length) return;`
- **Now:** `if (!result.files?.length) return;`
- **Crash:** `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')` if generator returns no `files` property

**Bug 2** — `runAITask` "completed" event (line 313)
- **Was:** `file_count: result.files.length,`
- **Now:** `file_count: result.files?.length ?? 0,`
- **Crash:** Same pattern — crashes if `result.files` is undefined during the completion event write

Both are the same root cause: assuming `result.files` is always present on `AITaskResult`. The generator could return an error result or malformed response without a `files` array, and either crash would leave the task in an inconsistent state instead of failing gracefully.

content.text:
/focus is not a recognized command. Here's what I can help with:

**Session controls:**
- `/reasoning` — toggle thinking mode on/off
- `/status` or `📊` — show session status card

**Available skills** (read with `read <path>`):
- `gortex-guide` — code intelligence tools
- `gortex-explore` — explore unfamiliar code
- `gortex-debug` — trace errors
- `gortex-impact` — safety analysis before changes
- `gortex-refactor` — rename, extract, restructure
- `diagram-maker` — create diagrams
- `spike` — feasibility prototypes
- `taskflow` — durable multi-step jobs
- And more — see full list in context

**What do you want to do?**

Successfully replaced 1 block(s) in /Users/asherlewis/PROJECTS/udd-web/lib/ai/service.ts.

Found and fixed a bug in `udd-web/lib/ai/service.ts` at line 357.

**The bug:** `persistFiles` used `if (!result.files.length) return;` without checking if `result.files` is defined. If the AI generator ever returns a result with no `files` property (e.g., an error result or malformed response), this throws `TypeError: Cannot read properties of undefined (reading 'length')`, crashing the task instead of failing gracefully.

**The fix:** Changed to `if (!result.files?.length) return;` — optional chaining safely handles undefined `files` by returning early, which is the intended no-op behavior for empty file sets.

**Why it matters:** This is in the critical path of every AI task completion. A single malformed generator response would leave the task hanging or unhandled instead of cleanly completing with no files.
